summary: Epic Dark Star marathon, soaring Here Comes Sunshine, tight China>Rider
blurb: The second set erupts with a massive Dark Star → Eyes Of The World → Stella Blue suite and a soaring Here Comes Sunshine. Keith Godchaux’s warm piano adds depth to the jams while Bill Kreutzmann drives the rhythm with relentless energy. Highlights include the classic China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition.
review:
December 6, 1973 at Cleveland Public Auditorium is remembered as a high‑energy ‘73 outing that truly shines in its second set. The band launches into an unforgettable Dark Star that morphs seamlessly into Eyes Of The World and then blossoms into Stella Blue, showcasing Jerry’s soaring guitar flights and Phil’s thunderous, melodic bass work. Here Comes Sunshine follows with a bright, lyrical solo from Garcia and tight interplay with Bob, while Keith Godchaux’s warm, crunchy piano textures add an extra layer of richness throughout the jams. The set is punctuated by a flawless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue that fans cite as one of the night’s most fluid transitions. Though the first set has a few slower moments, the overall performance is vibrant, inventive, and firmly places this show among the standout recordings of the early‑70s era.

Among the available sources, the Miller soundboard capture (gd1973-12-06.sbd.miller.105560.flac16) stands out for its crystal‑clear balance, “beautiful” tone, and complete, uninterrupted audio—making it the definitive listening experience for this legendary night.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recordings are marked positive; reviewers consistently praise Dark Star, Here Comes Sunshine, and the China Cat > Rider jam, with multiple mentions of soaring solos, tight rhythm section, and Keith's standout piano. No negative sentiment appears across analyses, justifying a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent Miller soundboard source praised as "beautiful" and "the best source ever," with full, uninterrupted audio and crystal‑clear instrument separation.

key_highlights: Monumental Dark Star → Eyes Of The World → Stella Blue suite
Tight China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition
Keith Godchaux’s warm, crunchy piano adding depth to the jams

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers soaring, lyrical solos on Dark Star and Here Comes Sunshine; vocal clarity shines on Ramble On Rose. A few reviewers note brief moments where Keith's piano outshines his playing.
band_performance.Phil: Acts as the MVP with thunderous, melodic bass that drives Dark Star and adds fuzzy, jazz‑fusion textures later in the set; occasional "bass bombs" lift the jams.
band_performance.Bob: Provides solid rhythm work and strong interplay on Here Comes Sunshine and Deal; later mix issues noted but his overall contribution remains sturdy.
band_performance.Keith: Warm, crunchy piano tones add depth and jazzy nuance, especially during Dark Star, Big River, and the China Cat > Rider jam; several reviewers claim his playing outshines Garcia at moments.
band_performance.Billy: Drums are "on fire," delivering a driving pulse throughout the second set and beating his kit to death on Big River.
